LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-COMPLAINANT STEVEN DAY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH CROSS-COMPLAINANT WAS SOMEWHAT DELINQUENT IN FILING THIS MOTION, AMENDMENTS ARE LIBERALLY GRANTED. THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO CROSS-DEFENDANT IF THERE IS A SHORT CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL. THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE OF JULY 9, 2012 IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 29 2012 AT 9:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 206. NOTICE WAS WAIVED BY THE PARTIES. ALL PARTIES HAVE UNTIL THIRTY DAYS BEFORE THE NEW TRIAL DATE TO COMPLETE DISCOVERY ON NEW ISSUES RAISED BY THE AMENDMENTS. CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS LEAVE TO TAKE ANOTHER DEPOSITION OF CROSS-COMPLAINANT LIMITED TO THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE AMENDMENTS. EXPERT DISCOVERY DEADLINES WILL BE TRIGGERED OFF THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ALL OTHER DISCOVERY AND PROCEDURAL DEADLINES REMAIN TRIGGERED BY THE ORIGINAL TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

Amended Notice Of Motion For Leave To Amend X-Complaint
Off Calendar May-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KIMBERLY A. FANADY, TEL. NO. 986-8467 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DUNCAN MACDONALD OF ROSSI & MACDONALD LLP, TEL. NO. (5100 522-7910 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-COMPLAINANT STEVEN DAY'S Amended Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint IS Granted. Although Cross-Complainant was somewhat delinquent in filing this motion, amendments are liberally granted. There is no prejudice to Cross-Defendant if there is a short continuance of the trial. The current trial date of July 9, 2012 is continued to October 29 2012 at 9:30am in Department 206. Notice was waived by the parties. All parties have until thirty days before the new trial date to complete discovery on new issues raised by the amendments. Cross-Defendant has leave to take another deposition of Cross-Complainant limited to the issues raised by the amendments. Expert discovery deadlines will be triggered off the new trial date. All other discovery and procedural deadlines remain triggered by the original trial date. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2011 Set For Trial On Jul-09-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Moton And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN M. KIPPERMAN, TEL. NO. 397-8600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KIMBERLY A. FANADY, TEL. NO. 986-8467 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DUNCAN MACDONALD & ATTY. ANNA M. ROSSI OF ROSSI & MACDONALD LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 522-7910 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT STEVEN DAY.

RULING - JUDGE KAHN MADE A DISCLOSURE REGARDING MR. KIPPERMAN WHO FOR SOME 25 YEARS AGO WAS AN ACQUAINTANCE AND HAD SOME FRIENDLY LUNCHES TOGETHER, BUT HAVE NO CONTACT WITH EACH OTHER FOR A LONG TIME NOW. HE DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF STRAUSS & SHAVELLE INCORPORATED'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is Granted. Plaintiff has shown that it is likely to succeed on its claim for breach of contract due to defendant's failure to return to plaintiff all of the documents that defendant was obligated to return under the parties' agreements. Plaintiff has also shown that it would suffer harm if the injunction is not granted. Defendant has not shown that he would be harmed in a material way by the injunction. Defendant is required to return only documents that he received while he worked as an employee or independent contractor of plaintiff, not any documents that he has obtained since he ceased working with plaintiff, including any documents he has received from his clients or obtained as a result of his own efforts. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK
